content: Welcome to Southeast Road Dogs. Drop your home base and your usual lanes when you get a chance.
Anthony out of Birmingham AL, mostly run I-65 and I-20. Thanks for the invite Ray.
Heads up, ice forming on I-40 through the Smokies tonight. Saw two rigs chained up near the NC line.
Confirmed, I-40 east of Knoxville is a mess. Slowed to 35 and the bridges are slick. Take it easy.
Appreciate the warning. Rerouting down I-59 instead, road's clear through Gadsden.
Big backup on I-75 south near Macon, wreck in the left two lanes. Lost about 40 minutes.
Scale house on I-65 north at Athens is open and they're pulling trucks in. Paperwork ready.
Weigh station on I-20 west at Anniston is closed tonight, green light all the way through.
Best fuel price I found today was the Pilot on I-65 exit 322. Lot was about half full at 6pm.
Anybody know if the TA on I-20 at Oxford still has overnight parking open? Rolling through late.
Yeah Anthony, TA at Oxford usually fills by 9 but there were spots when I left around 8.
If you want real BBQ stop at the diner off I-65 exit 318, brisket plate is worth the detour.
Can confirm on that brisket. Got the ribs last week too, good people and easy truck parking.
Heavy fog warning on I-40 west around Nashville before sunrise, visibility down to a quarter mile.
Severe storms rolling across I-20 through central GA this afternoon, watch for crosswinds.
Black ice advisory for the I-59 bridges near Fort Payne overnight. Slow down on the overpasses.
Construction zone on I-65 south at Cullman has it down to one lane, expect delays through the week.
Thanks Ray, hit that today. Crawled for about three miles but moving now. Clear past exit 308.
